review_text: This movie had me hooked the whole time

This is the only web of Films I feel as though I'm personally connected with not even just being Black or anything, but genuinely in how much it relates to the coming of age nowadays. Even moreso that me and Miles were the exact same ages in both films, part of me kind of hopes Miles figures it all out, cause I genuinely see myself in him so much, the taste in music in the first film and the change in tone of the second really making you feel and understand what weight being a teenager has both in expectations a constantly changing reality hormones and all that other nonsense. I adore how perceptive the Cinematics have been so far and can't wait for the finale. -BrklynSpdr

It tells a story that is very relatable to me, the way he's betrayed by someone he likes and or loves a friend, and he can't let his emotions out due to what he thinks his friends and family will think, and how he has to find himself and he is douted 24/7 and called a mistake. 10/10 critically and for casual moviegoers.

Movie was amazing, one of my fav animated movies of all time, to see a multiverse of all the different spiderman variants was awesome and broke the mold of what people perceived that spiderman story should be. It i definitely a movie I can watch over and over again and appreciate the art and details of the movie.

It took me way too long to finally watch this, and yeah—it’s a banger. The animation in the Spider-Verse movies is incredible, a total visual treat. On top of that, it’s just a really well-made film. The jokes are clever, the emotional moments actually landed for me, and I’m already counting down the days until the final movie. All in all, Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse isn’t just a great Spider-Man movie—it’s one of the best animated movies of all time.

I have nothing much to say that hasn't been said a million times, it's a brilliant, artful, awe-inspiring caper. I think maybe my brain does rebel a little against the style of animation as it's always trying to ground in reality what I see and that's not always possible. Surprised that it's a two-parter and that may annoy a lot of people but it's made me very keen to watch the next movie.

Spider-Man: Across The Spider-Verse, a great movie and easily the best animated movie of 2023. Miles Morales as a character. I can connect to him. I love some of the other Spider Men more specifically like Spider-Man 2099, Gwen Stacy, Spider Punk, they are also the highlights of this movie. The soundtrack is great, my favorite song was Calling by A Boogie With a Hoodie. The action sequences are so good and beautifully made. It is so creative. Now, my only problem was the ending of the movie and I understand it is a cliffhanger. But, it was disappointing. But, overall, I give this film a 9/10 or a 4/5 stars.

Great movie and a great sequel to the first entry. This movie shows the inner struggle within Miles and the hard choices he must make, clashing with his stubbornness and his will to save everyone he can, even if that happens to lead to more problems, which this movie shows very well. I loved Oscar Isaac’s performance as Miguel O’Hara and how well he captures the serious and focused tone of Spider-Man 2099. This movie is a step up in every aspect the first entry included, including the comedy, which I find to be funny at times, but it can come across as repetitive to some viewers, which is totally understandable. The dynamics between Miles, Peter B. Parker, and Gwen are also very well written, and how Miles feels like he’s been betrayed, even if their ultimate goal is for the greater good. Miles will be Miles and wants to save everyone he can. Ultimately, it’s a great movie that expands on everything we know and loved in the first entry. Absolute must-see!

The Spider-People wear different suits, but they share a similar mission and responsibility to discover what is truly valuable and strive to achieve it, and they cherish the belief that with great power comes great responsibility

Hermosa en todo sentido. Es una Endgame, es brillante y magnífica de ver. No entiendo como hay tantas personas que pueden decir que no es una obra de arte, literalmente los colores hablan en esta película... no, no hablan, GRITAN. Cuando Gwen habla con su papá, los colores son fríos y su papá tiene colores cálidos porque es una persona que intenta ser cálida en una situación tan complicada, al abrazarlo, todo se torna de colores cálidos por amor. Eso y mil otras partes de la película que hablan por sí mismo Y POR ÚLTIMOOOOO, EL CLIFFHANGER DEL FINAAAALLLLLL 400/10.

I’ll probably give this one at least 4.5 stars once Beyond the Spider-Verse comes out, but for now, its absence is kind of hurting this one for me. But this is still a great, highly expansive sequel that brings in a new batch of interesting characters, and progresses the story meaningfully all the way up until that cliffhanger ending that… really needs to be resolved.

Like the first film, the visuals are just out of this world. The complex, vivid animation amazes me every time. The writing is also very good. The story line grasped my attention and was interesting to watch. The cliffhanger, in my opinion, was also much needed. After watching the film in theaters, I was so excited for the next film's release. My only complaint is that they didn't release a third movie to conclude the story soon enough to build off the excitement this movie left us with.

Across The Spider-Verse is almost a perfect addition to the franchise as it adds an awesome character development to Miles, great references to the comics and Spidey adaptations we love, and a more serious tone - but in the right way to do it. Can't wait for the next one!!

With incredible animation, incredible action sequences, and an incredible soundtrack, Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse proves itself as one of the greatest movies of that year.

I love this movie so much!! I consider this movie peak cinema and lemme explain why. 1. The score really backed up those scenes in such a nice and impactful way. 2. The moral questions it forces you to think about are very mind-catching 3. The animation (of course) is very comic booky and smooth. 4. The soundtrack hits real hard, per usual for spider-verse so far!! 5. The character development and writing is really good!! Tying in the first movie in such an organic and smooth way, the character development of miles, gwen, and they're relationship together. Don't even get me started on the rio and miles scene on the water tower. All in all, I really like this movie and consider it my top 3 favorite movies of ALL TIME (specifically 1st place, kpop demon hunters 2nd, and 3rd would be Dawn of the planet of the apes). So 10/10 for me!!
